title: Fracture (Broken Bone)
immediate_action: Immobilize the injury. Do NOT try to realign the bone. Call emergency for open/compound fractures.

steps.instruction: Do NOT move the injured limb. Support it in the position found. If an open fracture (bone visible through skin), cover the wound with a clean dressing.
steps.tip: Open fractures are emergencies — bone exposed = infection risk. Keep it clean.

steps.instruction: Immobilize the fracture: splint the limb using rigid material (magazine, board, stick). Pad with soft material. Secure with bandages or cloth.
steps.tip: Splint should extend beyond joints above and below the fracture.

steps.instruction: Apply ice wrapped in cloth to reduce swelling. 20 minutes on, 20 minutes off.
steps.tip: NEVER apply ice directly to skin. Use a cloth barrier. Do not apply to open wounds.

steps.instruction: Elevate the injured limb above heart level if possible and not too painful.
steps.tip: Elevation reduces swelling. Support with pillows or cushions.

steps.instruction: Monitor for signs of shock (pale, sweaty, rapid pulse) and circulation below the fracture (cold/numb/blue fingers or toes). Loosen splint if circulation is compromised.
steps.tip: Check pulses, sensation, and movement in fingers/toes every 15 minutes.

when_to_stop: Person is transported safely to hospital
do_not: Do NOT try to push bone back in
Do NOT straighten a deformed limb
Do NOT apply ice directly to skin
Do NOT give food/water if surgery may be needed
